repo_notes.content: A control plane for concurrent LLM RL on shared GPUs

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce RLix, explain its purpose as a Ray-based control plane for concurrent LLM RL training with GPU time-sharing, and provide a high-level architectural summary

pages.title: What is RLix?
pages.purpose: Explain the problem RLix solves (underutilized GPUs during RL rollouts), core value proposition (multi-pipeline GPU time-sharing), and key features

pages.title: Architecture at a Glance
pages.purpose: Present high-level system architecture diagrams showing Control Plane, Pipelines, GPU Clusters, and their relationships

pages.title: Key Concepts
pages.purpose: Define fundamental terminology: pipelines, coordinators, GPU time-sharing, priority-based scheduling, LoRA adapters, rollout workers, and scheduling stages

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Guide users through initial setup, running their first pipeline, and understanding basic configuration

pages.title: Installation and Setup
pages.purpose: Cover dependencies, Ray cluster setup, environment variables, and initial configuration requirements

pages.title: Quick Start Example
pages.purpose: Walk through running start_multi_pipeline_test.py, explain the initialization sequence, and show expected output

pages.title: Configuration Basics
pages.purpose: Introduce Hydra configuration files, explain cluster_tp_configs and cluster_device_mappings, and show basic pipeline config structure

pages.title: Control Plane
pages.purpose: Document the singleton control plane components (Orchestrator, Scheduler, ResourceManager) that manage all pipelines

pages.title: Orchestrator
pages.purpose: Explain Orchestrator's role in pipeline lifecycle management: allocate_pipeline_id, register_pipeline, admit_pipeline, kill_pipeline, shutdown

pages.title: Scheduler
pages.purpose: Document SchedulerImpl's priority-based GPU allocation, request/release APIs, pending request queuing, and central scheduling loop

pages.title: Resource Manager
pages.purpose: Explain ResourceManager's role in GPU topology validation, cluster resource snapshots, and gpus-per-node enforcement

pages.title: Pipeline Registration Flow
pages.purpose: Detail the complete pipeline registration sequence: ID allocation → registration → topology validation → admission

pages.title: Pipeline Coordination
pages.purpose: Document the PipelineCoordinator actor's role in managing individual pipelines and coordinating with the control plane

pages.title: PipelineCoordinator Actor
pages.purpose: Explain PipelineCoordinator's initialization, configuration validation (CPU-only reward, vLLM sleep_level, offload_nccl), and environment variable injection

pages.title: Progress Reporting
pages.purpose: Document how PipelineCoordinator aggregates progress from sub-schedulers and reports to central Scheduler at 2% cadence

pages.title: Resize and Sync Coordination
pages.purpose: Explain how PipelineCoordinator serializes resize_infer and sync_lora_weights operations using locks to prevent race conditions

pages.title: Pipeline Actor Creation
pages.purpose: Detail how PipelineCoordinator determines pipeline type (LoRA vs full finetune), injects environment variables, and creates the pipeline actor

pages.title: Pipeline Implementations
pages.purpose: Document the two pipeline implementations: RollFullFinetunePipeline for full finetuning and RollMultiLoraPipeline for multi-LoRA training

pages.title: RollFullFinetunePipeline
pages.purpose: Explain full finetune pipeline's training loop, GPU request/release cycle, and integration with Rlix scheduler

pages.title: RollMultiLoraPipeline
pages.purpose: Document multi-LoRA pipeline's per-tag schedulers, first-ready dispatch model, isolated optimizers, and independent step tracking

pages.title: GPU Cluster Components
pages.purpose: Describe the five cluster types: actor_train, actor_infer, critic, reference, reward, and their roles in the RL pipeline

pages.title: Training Loop Phases
pages.purpose: Detail the sequential phases in the training loop: expand → rollout → shrink → old log probs → advantage → training → sync

pages.title: GPU Resource Management
pages.purpose: Explain how RLix achieves GPU time-sharing through priority-based scheduling, elastic workers, and background rebalancing

pages.title: Scheduling Priorities
pages.purpose: Document the six priority levels (0-6), explain which stages are preemptable, and how priority determines GPU allocation

pages.title: GPU Allocation Strategies
pages.purpose: Contrast full device_mapping allocation for training clusters vs gap-ratio DP allocation for generation clusters

pages.title: Elastic Rollout Workers
pages.purpose: Explain how actor_infer workers expand onto idle GPUs and shrink when higher-priority stages need capacity

pages.title: Background Rebalancing
pages.purpose: Document the demand-driven rebalancing mechanism that redistributes generation workers based on progress reports and demand/GPU imbalance

pages.title: Two-Level GPU Sharing
pages.purpose: Explain cross-pipeline sharing (elastic expand/shrink) and within-pipeline sharing (multi-LoRA adapters on shared base model)

pages.title: Model Synchronization
pages.purpose: Document the ModelUpdateService's selective weight sync mechanism for pushing trained weights to inference workers
